Hi Steph

We want to ensure we are providing high-quality, engaging sessions for your center. Our goal is to continue doing what works well and adapt any elements that may not be meeting your needs to better suit your center when delivering Mini Monstars sessions.

If you could please respond and let me know how everything is going, it would be greatly appreciated.

Hi Steph,

It was so nice speaking with you today, and I am thrilled you enjoyed your first session enough to lock in for the year.

I am happy to confirm that we have increased your session time to 50 minutes at a cost of $210 + GST per session. This will include 20 minutes for Junior Kindy and 30 minutes for Kindy. Please note that sessions will continue through school holidays.

I am also pleased to confirm you are all set with Coach Hannah, continuing fortnightly from your original start date. We are so glad to have you with us!
As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.


Kind Regards,
Katie🙂

I hope you’re having a lovely week! I’m reaching out to share a quick update regarding our upcoming sessions. Unfortunately, both Brooke and Rikki are currently unwell; Brooke is recovering from an ankle injury and Rikki is navigating some morning sickness.

We are so looking forward to welcoming new coaches soon, have others with potential availability coming up and hope to have Brooke back in action shortly. In the meantime, we’ll need to pause our sessions for the next two weeks. I’ll be sure to keep you updated along the way. We truly appreciate your warmth and understanding!

Kind Regards

Katie

spoke to Skye explaining Ricci pregnant and suffering morning sickness and the coach I had to cover her has just fractured her ankle so will need to skip Monday and i will give her an update next week,aacknowledge thewy have had a rough start due to Rikki's pregnancy but confirmed they were enjoying sessions and once we are sorted they will have a consistent coach as planned
really enjoying session kids loving it
they were happy but first session rescheduled due to medical, and they said they wanted to make sure it was Monday afternoons moving forward but really love the session and Rikki great with the kids

Hi  Skye 

I called earlier to follow up on your recent Mini Monstars session, but as I wasn't able to reach anyone for feedback, I thought I would follow up with an email.

We want to ensure we are providing high-quality, engaging sessions for your center. Our goal is to continue doing what works well and adapt any elements that may not be meeting your needs to better suit your center when delivering Mini Monstars sessions.

If you could please respond and let me know how everything is going, it would be greatly appreciated.

Kind Regards
Katie 🙂
Skye not available advised they would get her call me tomorrow said I would also follow up with an email

Hi Skye,

I’m happy to confirm the rescheduled time for your session at Skippys ELC - Frenchville! We have you all set for Tuesday, June 16, 2026, from 2:30 PM to 3:30 PM.

Reqeena  is looking forward to seeing you then. Thanks for coordinating this with us!

Kind RegardsKatie

Dear Skye 
We are incredibly excited to be running our program within your centre.
To ensure smooth communication moving forward, please note that our accounts team (accounts@minimonstars.com) will handle all invoicing and financial matters. For all other items, including scheduling and session feedback, I will be your primary point of contact.
Regarding our upcoming schedule, please see the confirmed session details below:Skippys ELC - FrenchvilleCommencing: June 8, 2026, 2:30 PM
02:30 PM-03:30 PMCoaches: Reqeena LailiRepeats: WeeklyWeek Activity:Sport
Cou;d you please reply with your desired room break down .
Breakdown ExampleNursery-10minToddlers 20minKindy 30min
We would love for the parents at your centre to stay connected with us, as we offer much more than just our standard incursions. If they are interested in learning more about our program, they can sign up for our email list here: https://www.minimonstars.net/contact.
Additionally, we have developed a dedicated app where children can continue their learning at home or engage in fun sessions during the day. You can find more information about the app here: https://www.minimonstars.net/mini-monstars-app.
As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.
I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.

Kind regardsKatie

Hi Robyn and Skye,

Thank you for the update.

I have updated our records with Skye's  as contact  for Frenchville.

Kind Regards
Katie

Hi Robyn,

It was so lovely to hear the great news about the approvals! I completely understand how busy things are for you, so I thought I’d send a quick email.

We are so excited to get started! Would Monday, June 8th work as a start date? This should give us plenty of time for the team inductions. I’ve attached Ricci and Brooke’s Blue Cards below for your records.

I’d love to chat soon to confirm details and discuss how you want to organize the rooms for our sessions. 0406934451🙂

Kind Regards
Katie

Hi Robyn,

I’m sorry we haven’t been able to connect over the phone lately I keep missing you, so I thought I'd email.

I’m reaching out to see if there’s any news regarding the approval and start dates for Skippys ELC at Gracemere and Frenchville. I would love to hear any updates you might have. 

Kind Regards

patterns.prevention:
• Assign a primary coach plus one backup coach to each centre, confirmed in writing before program start, with monthly check-ins on consistency.

• Audit all centres currently running to identify gaps between scheduled and actual coach attendance, then redistribute workload where coverage is fragmented.

• Set a weekly contact cadence with centre directors to flag scheduling conflicts, staff absences, or mergers that affect program delivery before cancellations occur.

• Implement a 48-hour notice requirement for any coach change or missed session, with automatic replacement coordinator activated to maintain continuity.

patterns.prevention:
• Implement weekly check-ins with all coaches at centres to assess engagement levels and identify performance issues before cancellation occurs.

• Create a backup coach protocol so centres always have a replacement option ready if their primary coach leaves or underperforms.

• Audit program content and equipment at each centre to ensure sessions are age-appropriate, challenging, and aligned with what parents expect.

• Establish clear communication with centres about coach transitions upfront—never indicate a coach is temporary without having a confirmed long-term replacement in place.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres that merged or consolidated operations to understand their internal staffing capacity and offer flexible partnership models (reduced frequency, specific skill gaps only).

• Schedule quarterly check-ins with active centres to identify budget pressures early and propose cost-saving alternatives before they explore other providers.

• Document positive coach feedback (e.g., "Eddie was amazing") and use it in retention conversations—highlight continuity and relationship value when centres consider switching.

• Audit competitor pricing and service offerings in your region to ensure your rates and program variety remain competitive against in-house alternatives.

Affinity  Group -direction to cut all incursions immediately at center as already over budget. waived 4 session cancellation to assist with confirmation in writing we will be preferred provider as soon as incursions are back email confirmation

Date Started: 28/11/2025
Number of Sessions Completed: 22
Sessions Missed: 0
Rescheduled: 0

Coaches: Mataus ,Patsy

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget

strong feedback regarding the value of the program and Coach Charlotte's development over time. However, there were several opportunities where we could have strengthened the relationship further. centres.closedDesc:
Reason for finishing: initial reason budget cuts and parents' choice on programs, had Noah for final 4 sessions but scheduled for 1:-4pm and time didn't work, called and emailed to reschedule time however they said they had other incursions on the mornings now so couldn't move time and couldn't recover in final 4 weeks

Katie Noah

Summary of their time with us:

came on 8th Oct 25 Didn't return in new year or cancel had to follow up and eventually Rhi got con tact for last 4 sessions however sessions set 1-4pm and they cancelled many of the last session as they said time didn't suit but also wouldn't change time, Noah also was a new coach and we stuggle to fiund coaches at that stage

  - Oct 8–10: Service commenced with positive feedback for older children.
  - Oct 16–24: Initial time changes were requested to avoid lunch clashes; however, feedback from the center contact, Liz, was limited as she did not work on session days.
  - Nov – Dec: Standard check-ins occurred. The center confirmed they were happy with the sessions, and the final date for 2025 was set for December 18th.

January – February 2026: Cancellation and Notice Period

  - Jan 19: Liz emailed to cancel the service due to head office budget cuts. I confirmed the four-week notice period, setting the final week for February 19th.
  - Feb 13–16: Efforts were made to save the account by offering different session types (music). I spoke with Sophie Jones to provide the formal four-session notice information.

March 2026: Scheduling Conflicts and Billing Disputes

  - Mar 5–9: Coach Noah reported significant issues; the center was unaware of his arrival, and the 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM slot clashed with nap times. I suggested moving the remaining sessions to a morning slot (8:30 AM – 11:30 AM).
  - Mar 12–18: Sophie Jones (Senior Manager) stated they could not accommodate the new times due to other bookings. She questioned if the final four weeks had been paid for or required an invoice.
  - Mar 23: The center cancelled the final session with Noah, citing the unsuitable time and their existing bookings.

Next point of contact:
Dollow up term 4
signals: AI reasoning: Centre cancelled due to budget, then booked other incursions on those timeslots instead. They switched providers rather than pause the program.
